content.full_text:
A-76-303

April , 1976

Mr. William F. Curran, Chairman
Nevada County Board of Supervisors
201 Church Street
Nevada City, California 95959

Dear Mr. Curran:

Your letter of March 11, 1976, to Chairman Daniel
Lowenstein of the Fair Political Practices Commission was
referred to me for reply.

I regret very much the resignation of a member of the
Nevada County Airport Commission because of anticipated
requirements that he disclose financial interests pursuant
to a Conflict of Interest Code adopted under Government
Code §87300 et seq. The earliest date that a Conflict of
Interest Code could become effective pursuant to the Act
is April 1, 1976. Prior to the adoption by the Board of
Supervisors of a Conflict of Interest Code for the Nevada
County Airport Commission, it is incumbent upon the Commission itself to determine which employees, officers,
or members of the Commission could make or participate
in making decisions which could materially financially
affect any financial interest. (Government Code §§87100,
87103 and 87300 and 87302(a))

It is only the financial interest of the members of the
Airport Commission which could be so materially financially
affected which must be disclosed. Of the Commission member
may not affect a financial interest in executing his official duties, the interest need not be disclosed.

Any officer, employee or member of the Commission who
feels that he or she may have to disclose more than is
required by the act may seek judicial review of the action
of the Board of Supervisors in compelling such over-disclosure
before a Superior Court (see Government Code §67308).

Mr. William F. Curran
April , 1976
Page Two


     In any event, it does not now appear that any member of
the Board of Directors of the Airport Commission of the County
of Nevada have been compelled to disclose pursuant to a Con-
flict of Interest Code.

     Again, I regret the resignation of a committee member
because of anticipated disclosure requirements.

     A copy of a pamphlet prepared in my office designed to
provide guidance in the development and preparation of
Conflict of Interest Codes is enclosed for your information.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Delbert L. Spurlock, Jr., Chief
                                        Conflicts of Interest Division

DLS:glb

Enclosure

cc:  Assemblyman Eugene Chappie
     Senator Randolph Collier
     Senator Clare Berryhill

COUNTY OF NEVADA
STATE OF CALIFORNIA                    BOARD OF SUPERVISORS

Ralph Euchanon, 1st District
Robert F. Wilder, 2nd District
Mike McEvoy, 3rd District
Eric W. Road, 4th District
William Curran, 5th District

Theo. A. Kohler, Jr.
Ex-Officio Clerk of the Board                    201 Church Street
                                                 NEVADA CITY, CALIFORNIA 95959
                                                 Phone: Area Code 916-265-2561 Ext. 210

                                                 March 11, 1976


Daniel Lowenstein, Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
926 "J" Street, Suite 522
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Lowenstein:

     At their meeting of March 9, 1976 the Nevada County Board of
Supervisors expressed their strong opposition and objection
to the mandated requirement whereby a financial statement
must be filed by persons serving in a totally voluntary
capacity in the service of the County.

     The enclosed copy of a letter of resignation received by the
Board of Supervisors from a volunteer committee member serving
on the Nevada County Airport Commission is an example that this
requirement is causing many resignations from conscientious
volunteer committee, commission and advisory board members who
give of their time and efforts to assist the County government
in its ultimate function.

     We urge the Commission's support in the elimination of the
aforementioned requirement.

                                                 Sincerely,

                                                 [signature]
                                                 WILLIAM F. CURRAN, CHAIRMAN
                                                 Nevada County Board of Supervisors
                                                 Supervisor - Fifth District

/jh

CC:  Assemblyman Eugene Chappie
     Senator Randolph Collier
     Senator Clare Berryhill

DON LITCHFIELD
General Contractor                    PHONE 273-9361              P. O. BOX 115
                                                                   ROUGH AND READY, CALIFORNIA 95975

                                                                   February 25, 1976


                                                                   RECEIVED

                                                                   FEB 27 1976

Board of Directors
Airport Commission
Nevada City, Calif. 95959

Gentlemen:

        It is with regret that I am hereby tendering
my resignation from the Board of Directors of the Air-
port Commission.

        I feel that the Statement which we must file
in order to comply with the provisions of the Financial
Disclosure Law (Proposition 9) is an invasion of privacy
and wholly unwarranted when one is serving in a totally
voluntary capacity.

        My resignation will be effective March 25th,
1976.

                                                    Yours truly,


                                                    DON LITCHFIELD
